---
title: "PREDICTS Metadata Extraction Pipeline"
subtitle: "Audit-first extraction from ecological papers into PV07 metadata"
format:
  html:
    theme: darkly
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    code-fold: true
    number-sections: false
    smooth-scroll: true
    output-file: index.html
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

<style>
.hero {
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #173f42, #243c32);
  border: 1px solid #3c6061;
  border-radius: 18px;
  padding: 2rem;
  margin: 1.5rem 0 2rem 0;
}
.hero h1, .hero h2, .hero h3 { margin-top: 0; }
.status-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(180px, 1fr));
  gap: 1rem;
  margin: 1rem 0 2rem 0;
}
.status-card {
  border: 1px solid #555;
  border-radius: 14px;
  padding: 1.1rem;
  background: #2b2b2b;
}
.status-card .big {
  font-size: 2rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  line-height: 1.1;
}
.status-card .label {
  color: #ccc;
  font-size: 0.95rem;
}
.flow {
  border: 1px solid #555;
  border-radius: 14px;
  padding: 1rem;
  background: #2b2b2b;
  font-size: 1rem;
}
.badge {
  display: inline-block;
  padding: 0.25rem 0.55rem;
  border-radius: 999px;
  border: 1px solid #777;
  margin: 0.15rem;
  background: #303030;
}
iframe.pdf-preview {
  width: 100%;
  height: 850px;
  border: 1px solid #666;
  border-radius: 10px;
  background: white;
}
</style>

# What this page shows

::: {.hero}
This page documents the current prototype pipeline for extracting metadata from ecological papers and preparing PREDICTS (Projecting Responses of Ecological Diversity in Changing Terrestrial Systems) PV07 forms.

The main aim is **auditability**: each filled field should be traceable to evidence from the paper text, Methods section, tables, maps, OCR/vision output, or downloaded repository data.

This is **not presented as fully automatic yet**. It is an audit-first workflow where difficult fields such as **site counts, species counts, dates, coordinates and study splitting** are flagged for human validation.
:::

# Current status

<div class="status-grid">
  <div class="status-card"><div class="big">58</div><div class="label">test PDFs in the current batch</div></div>
  <div class="status-card"><div class="big">2</div><div class="label">worked examples documented here</div></div>
  <div class="status-card"><div class="big">11</div><div class="label">pipeline stages with audit outputs</div></div>
  <div class="status-card"><div class="big">PV07</div><div class="label">filled PDF form generated per paper</div></div>
</div>

The current workflow demonstrates:

- PDF text extraction;
- DOI and citation extraction;
- Docling table extraction;
- OCR and optional vision-model evidence;
- repository download handling, including Dryad ZIPs;
- nested archive extraction;
- metadata JSON production;
- filled PREDICTS PV07 PDF form generation.

The main remaining work is quality control: the pipeline must still be checked for **site counts, species counts, dates, coordinates, country names and whether a paper should be split into multiple PV07 studies**.

# Why this pipeline is needed

Ecological papers rarely report all PV07 fields in one clean table. Important information is often split across:

- the title page;
- abstract;
- Methods and study-area sections;
- maps and figure captions;
- model/result tables;
- supplementary files;
- data availability statements;
- README files inside downloaded archives.

A robust workflow therefore needs to combine deterministic extraction rules, repository download logic, table parsing, OCR/vision evidence and cautious LLM fallback. The LLM is used only for missing or weak fields and should not overwrite stronger deterministic evidence.

# Pipeline at a glance

<div class="flow">
PDF paper → extract text and DOI/citation metadata → extract Docling tables → discover supplementary/data links → download files and recursively unzip archives → parse CSV/XLSX/TXT/JSON/PDF evidence → export useful pages as images → OCR and optional vision analysis → deterministic metadata extraction → optional LLM fallback for missing/weak fields → save master JSON and evidence workbook → fill the PREDICTS PV07 PDF form
</div>

# Evidence sources

| Evidence source | Why it is used | Example PV07 fields |
|---|---|---|
| Main PDF text | Highest-value source for study design, Methods, dates and location descriptions | title, abstract, country, study description, sampling method, dates |
| Docling tables | Extracts structured tables embedded in the article PDF | site counts, species/group counts, metric/unit evidence |
| OCR | Reads text embedded in page images, maps and scanned tables | coordinates, site labels, captions, map text |
| Vision model | Optional interpretation of visual maps/plots/tables | coordinate ranges, map-derived site evidence |
| Repository downloads | Reads CSV, Excel, ZIP, TXT, JSON, PDF and README files | site counts, species counts, coordinates, years, abundance units |
| LLM fallback | Only used when deterministic evidence is missing or weak | ambiguous descriptions and comments |

# Output produced per paper

| Output | Description |
|---|---|
| `text_metadata.json` | Page-aware PDF text extraction and text-derived evidence |
| `doi_metadata.json` | DOI and citation extraction |
| `pdf_tables/` | Docling tables exported as CSV/XLSX/HTML |
| `supplementary_files/` | Downloaded repository or publisher files |
| `extracted_data/` | Parsed tables from downloaded files and unzipped archives |
| `pdf_images/` | Page images selected for OCR/vision |
| `image_extracted_data/` | OCR text, vision JSON, map/coordinate CSV evidence |
| `evidence_used_for_form.xlsx` | Human-readable evidence workbook |
| `master_metadata.json` | Final metadata object used for filling |
| `*_filled_predicts.pdf` | Filled PV07 PDF form |
| `field_map.json` | Mapping between metadata fields and PDF form fields |

# Repository download logic

The downloader is deliberately redundant because publisher PDFs often break URLs or repository DOIs across lines.

<span class="badge">Dryad</span>
<span class="badge">Figshare</span>
<span class="badge">Zenodo</span>
<span class="badge">EIDC/CEH</span>
<span class="badge">OSF</span>
<span class="badge">publisher supplements</span>
<span class="badge">Crossref related links</span>

For Dryad, the key requirement is to avoid stopping at the landing page. The code must resolve the dataset page to actual file downloads, save ZIP files, recursively unzip them, and parse the CSV/XLSX files inside.

# Example 1: Jarrett et al. 2021

## Why this example matters

Jarrett tests whether the pipeline can use large repository CSV files and handle conflicts between article-level design counts and downloaded table counts.

## Extracted metadata summary

| Field | Extracted value |
|---|---|
| First author | Jarrett |
| Year | 2021 |
| Study system | African cocoa agroforestry bird communities |
| Countries | Cameroon; Equatorial Guinea |
| Sampling method | Mist-netting |
| Article-reported sites | 83 |
| Repository evidence | `CaptureDataset.csv`, `CocoaFarmTreeCover.csv`, `BirdCharacteristics.csv` |
| Date evidence | repository year column, 1990–2020 |

## QC points

| Issue | Interpretation | Action |
|---|---|---|
| Repository tables give different site/species counts | Different files represent different sampling units and historical datasets | Keep article design count, but log repository counts as evidence |
| Country field can appear as “Multiple countries” | PV07 may need explicit names or comments | Record Cameroon and Equatorial Guinea in comments/evidence |
| Species count conflict | Repository species columns may be stronger than generic PDF phrases | Flag for human review before final PV07 entry |

# Example 2: Kingazi et al. 2024

## Why this example matters

Kingazi tests two common failure modes: a Dryad page with a downloadable ZIP, and a paired sampling design that can be misread as either one broad study area or two treatments.

## Extracted metadata summary

| Field | Extracted value |
|---|---|
| First author | Kingazi |
| Year | 2024 |
| Title | Tropical agroforestry supports insect pollinators and improves bean yield |
| Journal | Journal of Applied Ecology |
| DOI | `10.1111/1365-2664.14629` |
| Country | Tanzania |
| Study area | Eastern lower slopes of Mount Kilimanjaro; Kilimanjaro Region; Northern Tanzania; Chagga home gardens |
| Sampling method | Fixed plots/quadrats |
| Site count used in filled form | 16 paired sampling sites/replicates |
| Plot-level repository evidence | 32 plot IDs in pollinator/NMDS files |
| Study dates | March–May 2022 |
| Number of species | 45 |

## Dryad archive evidence

Kingazi has a Dryad data page with one ZIP. Inside that ZIP are the usable CSV files:

- `Bean yield data.csv`
- `Insect pollinator data.csv`
- `NMDS_Data.csv`

The downloader must save the ZIP, unzip it, and parse the CSVs inside. A shallow downloader will incorrectly report zero usable data files.

## Important site-count interpretation

For the PV07 form, this example currently records **16** sites/paired sampling replicates, because the article describes **16 agroforestry plots paired with 16 non-agroforestry plots**. The downloaded data also contains 32 plot-level records, representing the two treatment plots within the 16 paired design.

This distinction is important: the pipeline should not mistake the two treatment classes for two sites, and it should not collapse the whole study map into one site.

# Filled PV07 form example

[Open the filled Kingazi PV07 PDF](examples/JM1_2024__Kingazi/JM1_2024__Kingazi_filled_predicts.pdf)

<iframe class="pdf-preview" src="examples/JM1_2024__Kingazi/JM1_2024__Kingazi_filled_predicts.pdf"></iframe>

# Cross-example comparison

| Feature | Jarrett et al. 2021 | Kingazi et al. 2024 |
|---|---|---|
| Taxon/group | Birds | Insect pollinators and bean yield |
| Study system | Cocoa agroforestry | Chagga home gardens / bean plots |
| Countries | Cameroon; Equatorial Guinea | Tanzania |
| Main repository challenge | Multiple large CSV files; counts differ by file purpose | Dryad ZIP containing nested usable CSV files |
| Strongest evidence for dates | Repository year column | Methods and data files |
| Strongest evidence for sites | Article Methods/design statement | Methods: 16 paired sampling units; repository: 32 plot IDs |
| Main QC risk | Species/site counts from different files conflict | Treatment/plot count mistaken for site count |

# Validation rules before filling PV07

| Rule | Rationale |
|---|---|
| Prefer explicit Methods design counts over generic counts found in result tables | Model tables often contain treatments, not sites |
| Prefer repository coordinates over map OCR when coordinate columns exist | CSV coordinates are usually more precise |
| Do not overwrite article-reported site counts with unrelated unique IDs unless the table is clearly the sampling-unit table | Unique IDs can represent visits, plots, farms, stations or records |
| Keep a conflict log when PDF text, tables and repository data disagree | Human review is essential for PREDICTS |
| Recursively unzip archives | Many repositories package files in nested folders or ZIPs |
| Treat LLM output as fallback only | Prevents hallucinated fields and invalid dropdown values |
| Store final field value plus source evidence | Makes the filled PDF auditable |

# Known issues and fixes in progress

| Issue found during testing | Current fix/status |
|---|---|
| Dryad DOI landing pages were found but the actual ZIP was not downloaded | Dryad resolver patched to follow dataset/download/file-stream links |
| Dryad share links, such as Betemariyam, were not downloaded | Share-link handling added separately |
| Illegal OCR characters crashed Excel evidence workbook writing | Excel sanitisation added |
| Kingazi site count was misread | Paired-design logic added; currently records 16 paired sampling replicates |
| Repository-derived species counts can conflict with PDF-derived counts | Needs explicit QC flags and source-priority rules |
| Multi-country or multi-study papers may need splitting | Needs human validation and future study-splitting logic |

# Recommended next steps

1. Add `qc_flags` to `master_metadata.json` for site/species/date/coordinate conflicts.
2. Add `site_count_source`, `species_count_source` and `date_source` fields.
3. Add a compact HTML report per paper showing final metadata, evidence files and QC flags.
4. Add unit tests for repository download functions using known Dryad, Figshare, Zenodo, EIDC and OSF examples.
5. Agree with the NHM/PREDICTS team how paired designs should be recorded in PV07: paired replicate count, plot count, or separate studies.

# Minimal command-line example

```bash
find ../Papers2Test -iname "*.pdf" | while read paper; do
  python predicts_form.py \
    --paper "$paper" \
    --form PREDICTS_Form_v07.pdf \
    --compiler-first Joana \
    --compiler-last Medeiros \
    --compiler-id JM1 \
    --compiler-email j.medeiros@pgr.reading.ac.uk \
    --download-dir ./downloaded_data \
    --model gemma3:4b \
    --vision-model moondream \
    --vision-max-pages 15 \
    --max-ocr-pages 15
done
```
